text.tspan: FEDERATED IDENTITY: your app does NOT manage passwords.
It redirects to a TRUSTED IDENTITY PROVIDER (Google, GitHub, Okta, Azure AD, etc.)
which authenticates the user and returns a verifiable token.
USER
(has Google / GitHub / Okta / corporate AD account)
YOUR APPLICATION (RELYING PARTY)
no password database
no MFA implementation
no password-reset flows
no compromised-credential checks
just: verify a token from the IdP and trust the user identity
IDENTITY PROVIDER (IdP)
Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Apple
Okta, Auth0, Ping, OneLogin
Azure AD, Google Workspace, Active Directory
Keycloak, Authelia, Authentik (self-hosted)
ALL the actual security work happens here:
passwords, MFA, biometrics, anomaly detection,
compromised-credential checks, password rotation, etc.
TOKEN (JWT / SAML assertion)
signed by the IdP u2192 your app verifies the signature
contains: user identifier, name, email, roles, expiry
short-lived (minutes to hours); refresh token used to renew
cryptographic guarantee that the IdP authenticated the user
WHY FEDERATE
u2022 you don't store passwords u2192 you can't leak them
u2022 you get MFA / hardware keys for free (the IdP implements)
u2022 enterprise SSO (one corporate login for all SaaS)
u2022 specialist security teams (Google, Okta) do the hard work
u2022 user joins/leaves one place u2192 access updated everywhere
u2022 standards-based (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, SAML)
WHEN NOT TO FEDERATE
u2022 air-gapped / no-internet systems
u2022 zero-dependency requirements
u2022 specific compliance that mandates internal authentication
u2022 but for nearly all consumer/enterprise SaaS u2192 FEDERATE
